controls.title: Threat intelligence
controls.description: Information relating to information security threats is collected and analysed to produce actionable threat intelligence.

controls.title: Authentication information
controls.description: Allocation and management of authentication information is controlled, including advising personnel on proper handling.

controls.title: Information security for cloud services
controls.description: Acquisition, use, management and exit from cloud services are governed by the organization's information security requirements.

controls.title: ICT readiness for business continuity
controls.description: ICT readiness is planned, implemented, maintained and tested based on business continuity objectives.

controls.title: Information security awareness & training
controls.description: Personnel receive appropriate security awareness education and training relevant to their role and function.

controls.title: Physical security monitoring
controls.description: Premises are continuously monitored for unauthorized physical access.

controls.title: Secure authentication
controls.description: Secure authentication technologies and procedures are implemented based on access restrictions and the access control policy.

controls.title: Management of technical vulnerabilities
controls.description: Information about technical vulnerabilities is obtained, the organization's exposure evaluated, and appropriate measures taken.

controls.title: Logging
controls.description: Logs recording activities, exceptions, faults and other relevant events are produced, stored, protected and analysed.

controls.title: Monitoring activities
controls.description: Networks, systems and applications are monitored for anomalous behaviour to evaluate potential security incidents.

controls.title: Web filtering
controls.description: Access to external websites is managed to reduce exposure to malicious content.

controls.title: Use of cryptography
controls.description: Rules for the effective use of cryptography, including key management, are defined and implemented.

controls.title: Secure development life cycle
controls.description: Rules for the secure development of software and systems are established and applied.

controls.title: Application security requirements
controls.description: Information security requirements are identified, specified and approved when developing or acquiring applications.

controls.title: Secure coding
controls.description: Secure coding principles are applied to software development to reduce vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Broken access control
controls.description: Restrictions on what authenticated users may do are not properly enforced, allowing access to unauthorized functions or data.

controls.title: Cryptographic failures
controls.description: Sensitive data is inadequately protected in transit or at rest due to missing or weak cryptography.

controls.title: Injection
controls.description: Untrusted data is sent to an interpreter as part of a command or query, allowing execution of unintended commands (SQL, OS, XSS).

controls.title: Insecure design
controls.description: Missing or ineffective security controls arising from flaws in design and architecture rather than implementation.

controls.title: Security misconfiguration
controls.description: Insecure default configurations, incomplete setups, or verbose error messages expose the application to attack.

controls.title: Vulnerable & outdated components
controls.description: Use of components with known vulnerabilities, or that are unsupported and out of date.

controls.title: Identification & authentication failures
controls.description: Weaknesses in authentication and session management that allow account compromise.

controls.title: Software & data integrity failures
controls.description: Code and infrastructure that fail to protect against integrity violations, e.g. insecure deserialization or unsigned updates.

controls.title: Security logging & monitoring failures
controls.description: Insufficient logging, detection and response that lets breaches go unnoticed.

controls.title: Server-side request forgery (SSRF)
controls.description: The application fetches a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URL, enabling requests to unintended destinations.

controls.title: Organizational context
controls.description: The circumstances surrounding the organization's cybersecurity risk management decisions are understood.

controls.title: Risk management strategy
controls.description: Priorities, constraints, risk tolerance and assumptions are established and used to support operational risk decisions.

controls.title: Asset management
controls.description: Assets (data, hardware, software, systems, people) are identified and managed consistent with their risk to objectives.

controls.title: Risk assessment
controls.description: The cybersecurity risk to the organization, its assets and individuals is understood.

controls.title: Identity management, authentication & access control
controls.description: Access to assets is limited to authorized users, services and hardware, and managed commensurate with risk.

controls.title: Data security
controls.description: Data is managed consistent with the organization's risk strategy to protect confidentiality, integrity and availability.

controls.title: Platform security
controls.description: The hardware, software and services of physical and virtual platforms are managed consistent with risk.

controls.title: Technology infrastructure resilience
controls.description: Security architectures are managed to protect asset confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience.

controls.title: Continuous monitoring
controls.description: Assets are monitored to find anomalies, indicators of compromise and other potentially adverse events.

controls.title: Adverse event analysis
controls.description: Anomalies, indicators of compromise and other adverse events are analysed to characterize them and detect incidents.

controls.title: Incident management
controls.description: Responses to detected cybersecurity incidents are managed.

controls.title: Incident recovery plan execution
controls.description: Restoration activities are performed to ensure operational availability of systems and services affected by an incident.
